forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+---
+depends_on:
+- 01KWMS1F9CC0XYB0Q446930PBX
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9280'
+title: 'Examples: EchoTool, FileAssistant, ToolPicking, RemoteHTTP'
+---
+## What
+Create the first four `Examples/` executable targets per plan.md Examples Â§1â€“4, each runnable via `swift run <Name>` and compiled in CI: **EchoTool** (~20-line hello world: stdio echo server â†’ MCPServer â†’ LanguageModelSession(mcp:) on the system model â†’ one prompt, one tool call); **FileAssistant** (stdio filesystem server, multi-tool selection, isError bubbling on a missing-file prompt); **ToolPicking** (one loose MCPTool + a native Swift Tool composed in one session â€” MCPToolProvider flattening); **RemoteHTTP** (HTTPClientTransport + host-supplied bearer token, demonstrating the delegated-auth decision).
+
+- [ ] EchoTool
+- [ ] FileAssistant
+- [ ] ToolPicking
+- [ ] RemoteHTTP
+- [ ] All four wired into CI as build targets
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `swift build --target <Name>` succeeds for all four locally and in CI
+- [ ] Each example's source has a header comment stating what it demonstrates, matching plan.md
+- [ ] Model-dependent runtime paths guard on SystemLanguageModel availability with a clean message (examples must not crash on CI machines without the model)
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] CI job builds all four example targets (compilation is the automated gate)
+- [ ] Non-model helper logic (e.g. RemoteHTTP token injection) covered by unit tests in `Tests/FoundationModelsMCPTests/ExampleHelperTests.swift`
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` â€” write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

+Create the first four `Examples/` executable targets per plan.md Examples Â§1â€“4, each runnable via `swift run <Name>` and compiled in CI. **Toy servers come from the `MCPTestServer` utility target** (spawned as a stdio subprocess in the matching mode â€” echo mode for EchoTool, filesystem-style multi-tool mode for FileAssistant); examples never import the test target. Targets: **EchoTool** (~20-line hello world: spawn MCPTestServer echo â†’ MCPServer â†’ LanguageModelSession(mcp:) on the system model â†’ one prompt, one tool call); **FileAssistant** (multi-tool mode, model selects among tools, isError bubbling on a missing-file prompt); **ToolPicking** (one loose MCPTool + a native Swift Tool composed in one session â€” MCPToolProvider flattening); **RemoteHTTP** (HTTPClientTransport + host-supplied bearer token â€” the delegated-auth decision).

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,4 +1,34 @@
 ---
+comments:
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kwr5fwykp7gt2xgy39bsz915
+  text: |-
+    Implementation complete, build/test green. Summary:
+
+    Created:
+    - `Examples/EchoTool/EchoTool.swift`, `Examples/FileAssistant/FileAssistant.swift`, `Examples/ToolPicking/ToolPicking.swift`, `Examples/RemoteHTTP/RemoteHTTP.swift` â€” four `@main`-based executable targets (not `main.swift` top-level scripts, deliberately, so the test target can `@testable import` them for helper-logic coverage).
+    - `Examples/Support/` â€” new `ExampleSupport` library target shared by the four examples: `ExampleServerProcess.swift` (spawns `MCPTestServerCLI` as a subprocess, mirroring `E2ETests.swift`'s Process+Pipe+StdioTransport pattern), `ExampleModelGuard.swift` (`checkSystemLanguageModelAvailable`), `ConnectedExampleServer.swift` (`connectExampleServer`/`requireExampleServer` combining guard+spawn+connect).
+    - `Sources/MCPTestServer/ServerMode.swift` (new) â€” `ServerMode` enum (echo/filesystem/all) with `parse(from:)` + `registerTools(on:)`, so `MCPTestServerCLI` can select its tool set via `--mode`. `.all` (default, no flag) preserves the CLI's original register-everything behavior, so `E2ETests.swift` (which spawns with no args) is unaffected.
+    - `Sources/MCPTestServerCLI/main.swift` updated to use `ServerMode.parse`.
+    - `Package.swift` updated: added `ExampleSupport` + 4 example `executableTarget`s (explicit `path:` under `Examples/`), a shared `foundationModelsLinkerSettings` constant (was 6x duplicated `.linkedFramework("FoundationModels")` literal), and added `ExampleSupport`/`RemoteHTTP` to the test target's dependencies (via SwiftPM's test-target-imports-@main-executable-target support â€” not `@testable import` of the fixture target).
+    - `.github/workflows/ci.yml` â€” added a "Build Examples" step running `swift build --target <Name>` for each of the four.
+    - Tests: `Tests/FoundationModelsMCPTests/ServerModeTests.swift` (9 tests, TDD'd) and `Tests/FoundationModelsMCPTests/ExampleHelperTests.swift` (9 tests, TDD'd) covering ServerMode parsing/registration, ExampleServerProcess's executable-locating logic, the model-availability guard, `requireExampleServer`'s nil-when-unavailable branch, and RemoteHTTP's bearer-token `requestModifier`.
+
+    Design judgment calls:
+    1. **ToolPicking cannot use `LanguageModelSession(mcp:)`'s variadic init for the native tool** â€” that initializer only accepts `MCPToolProvider`-conforming values (only `MCPTool`/`MCPServer` conform per the completed MCPToolProvider task), and a plain `FoundationModels.Tool` doesn't conform. plan.md's illustrative `LanguageModelSession(mcp: clockTool, readFileTool)` snippet doesn't match the delivered API surface. Resolved by having ToolPicking call `resolveSessionTools(from:)` directly (the same flattening function the `mcp:` convenience wraps) on the one loose `MCPTool`, then appending the native `ClockTool` and constructing the session via the base `LanguageModelSession(tools:instructions:)` initializer â€” documented inline in ToolPicking.swift's header doc comment. Did not modify MCPToolProvider itself (out of scope, already reviewed/done).
+    2. **Examples use `@main` structs, not `main.swift` top-level scripts** â€” required so the test target can import them for `ExampleHelperTests.swift`/testable coverage of non-model helper logic, per SwiftPM's "test targets can depend on @main executable targets" support.
+    3. **`ExampleServerProcess` marked `@unchecked Sendable`** (wraps Foundation's non-Sendable `Process`) and `ConnectedExampleServer: Sendable` â€” needed to satisfy Swift 6 strict concurrency when calling the aggregate's own async `shutdown()` method from a `defer` in `@main`'s isolated context; each use is self-contained (no concurrent shared mutation), so `@unchecked` is safe.
+    4. Added a shared `requireExampleServer` combining the model-availability guard with spawn+connect after an initial review pass flagged the guard and connect-plumbing as 3x-duplicated across EchoTool/FileAssistant/ToolPicking.
+
+    Verification commands run (all green):
+    - `swift build` (clean, zero warnings)
+    - `swift build --target EchoTool` / `FileAssistant` / `ToolPicking` / `RemoteHTTP` (all succeed individually)
+    - `swift test` â†’ 196/196 tests pass, 22 suites
+    - `mcp__sah__review review working` â†’ 0 findings after two fix rounds (first round flagged duplicated model-guard/spawn-connect logic across the three subprocess examples plus the linker-literal duplication in Package.swift; both fixed)
+    - Adversarial double-check agent dispatched for final sign-off (in progress as of this comment)
+
+    Leaving task in `doing` per /implement workflow â€” not moving to review myself.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-05T03:34:58.259969+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KWMS1F9CC0XYB0Q446930PBX
 - 01KWMSDVP4JM77YR0YMCE9S5ME

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -29,6 +29,26 @@

     Leaving task in `doing` per /implement workflow â€” not moving to review myself.
   timestamp: 2026-07-05T03:34:58.259969+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kwr80ef3p1a3yb1waswc6ccm
+  text: |-
+    Adversarial double-check completed with verdict REVISE â€” one legitimate gap found: no regression test for the "Examples never import the test target" constraint going forward (this codebase already has `PackageDependencyTests.libraryTargetExcludesTestServer` for the analogous library-target case, but nothing equivalent was added for the new `ExampleSupport`/`EchoTool`/`FileAssistant`/`ToolPicking`/`RemoteHTTP` targets or the `Examples/` directory). No other findings â€” build, tests, doc comments, target wiring, CI step, and all four example flows were independently verified correct (double-check ran its own clean `swift build`/`swift test`, read every new/changed file end to end, and traced `HTTPClientTransport.requestModifier` through the swift-sdk source to confirm it's genuinely invoked per-request).
+
+    Fixed the gap: extended `Tests/FoundationModelsMCPTests/PackageDependencyTests.swift` with:
+    - `exampleTargetExcludesTestServer(targetName:)` (parameterized over the 5 new targets) â€” same textual `targetDeclaration(named:in:)` check already used for the library target.
+    - `exampleTargetDependenciesConstantExcludesTestServer()` â€” needed because a follow-up round of `review working` (see below) had me extract a shared `exampleTargetDependencies` constant that the four example targets now reference by name rather than spelling out inline; this closes the resulting gap where a regression added to that *shared* constant wouldn't show up in any individual target's own declaration text.
+    - `examplesNeverImportMCPTestServer()` / `examplesNeverUseTestableImport()` â€” recursively scans every `.swift` file under `Examples/` for `import MCPTestServer` / `@testable import`.
+
+    Then ran three more `review working` rounds to convergence (0 findings), fixing along the way:
+    - Extracted `mcpProduct`/`exampleTargetDependencies` shared `Package.swift` constants (the `.product(name: "MCP", package: "swift-sdk")` literal was repeated 8x; the 3-element example-target dependency array was repeated 4x).
+    - Added a top-level doc comment on `Package.swift`'s `package` declaration.
+    - Inlined two single-call-site test/helper functions the reviewer flagged as needless indirection (`writeExecutableStub` in ExampleHelperTests.swift, `productsDirectoryURL()` in ExampleServerProcess.swift â€” folded into `executableURL(productsDirectory:)`'s default parameter expression).
+    - Kept `ExampleServerProcess.launchArguments(forMode:)` as a standalone function despite one reviewer round flagging it as a "needless single-call-site helper" â€” it has two real call sites (the production `spawn(mode:)` call plus a direct unit test in `ExampleHelperTests.launchArgumentsPassesMode()`), and removing it would eliminate exactly the kind of testable "server-spawn plumbing" seam this task's Tests section explicitly asked for. Logged here as the justification for not acting on that one finding.
+
+    Final state: `swift package clean && swift build` (full clean rebuild, zero warnings) and `swift test` â†’ 201/201 tests pass across 22 suites. All four `swift build --target <Name>` (EchoTool/FileAssistant/ToolPicking/RemoteHTTP) still succeed individually. `review working` â†’ 0 findings.
+
+    Task remains in `doing`, ready for `/review`.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-05T04:18:57.635773+00:00

 - 01KWMS1F9CC0XYB0Q446930PBX
 - 01KWMSDVP4JM77YR0YMCE9S5ME

+  text: |-
+    Fixed review finding: collapsed the four near-identical `.executableTarget()` blocks (EchoTool, FileAssistant, ToolPicking, RemoteHTTP) in Package.swift into a data table + `.map()`, per the reviewer's specific instruction that Package.swift is regular Swift executed once at manifest resolution, so building the `targets:` array via `.map()` over a literal array of (name, path) is fine.
+
+    Changes:
+    - Added `exampleTargetSpecs: [(name: String, path: String)]` â€” the four (name, path) pairs, with a doc comment carrying forward what each example demonstrates (previously one comment per `.executableTarget()` block).
+    - Added `exampleTargets: [Target] = exampleTargetSpecs.map { ... }` generating all four `.executableTarget()` declarations from the shared `exampleTargetDependencies`/`foundationModelsLinkerSettings` constants (kept as-is, per the task instructions â€” this fix only collapses the four block declarations themselves).
+    - Wired `exampleTargets` into the `targets:` array via `[...] + exampleTargets + [...]` (still a fully resolved `[Target]` array, no I/O, deterministic).
+
+    Fallout fixed: `Tests/FoundationModelsMCPTests/PackageDependencyTests.swift`'s `exampleTargetExcludesTestServer(targetName:)` parameterized test previously located each of the four targets by a textual `name: "<targetName>",` search inside a `.target(`/`.executableTarget(` call â€” that text no longer exists for the four generated targets (their names now come from `spec.name`, not a literal). Updated the test file:
+    - `exampleTargetNames` now only contains `"ExampleSupport"` (still a literal declaration), with a doc comment explaining why the other four moved.
+    - Added `exampleTargetSpecsDeclaresExpectedNames()` â€” asserts `exampleTargetSpecs` contains all four expected name literals.
+    - Added `exampleTargetsMapUsesSharedDependencies()` â€” asserts the `exampleTargets` map body wires `dependencies: exampleTargetDependencies` (already proven MCPTestServer-free by the existing `exampleTargetDependenciesConstantExcludesTestServer()`) and doesn't reference `MCPTestServer` directly.
+
+    Verification (all green):
+    - `swift build` â€” clean, zero warnings
+    - `swift package clean && swift build` â€” full clean rebuild, zero warnings
+    - `swift test` â€” 203/203 tests pass, 22 suites
+    - `swift build --target EchoTool` / `FileAssistant` / `ToolPicking` / `RemoteHTTP` â€” all succeed individually
+    - `swift package describe --type json` â€” confirms all four targets resolve as `executable` at their correct `Examples/<Name>` paths
+    - `mcp__sah__review review working` â€” 0 findings
+
+    Checked off all four review-finding checklist items. Leaving task in `doing` for `/review`.

-
-    Changes:
-    - Added `exampleTargetSpecs: [(name: String, path: String)]` â€” the four (name, path) pairs, with a doc comment carrying forward what each example demonstrates (previously one comment per `.executableTarget()` block).
-    - Added `exampleTargets: [Target] = exampleTargetSpecs.map { ... }` generating all four `.executableTarget()` declarations from the shared `exampleTargetDependencies`/`foundationModelsLinkerSettings` constants (kept as-is, per the task instructions â€” this fix only collapses the four block declarations themselves).
-    - Wired `exampleTargets` into the `targets:` array via `[...] + exampleTargets + [...]` (still a fully resolved `[Target]` array, no I/O, deterministic).
-
-    Fallout fixed: `Tests/FoundationModelsMCPTests/PackageDependencyTests.swift`'s `exampleTargetExcludesTestServer(targetName:)` parameterized test previously located each of the four targets by a textual `name: "<targetName>",` search inside a `.target(`/`.executableTarget(` call â€” that text no longer exists for the four generated targets (their names now come from `spec.name`, not a literal). Updated the test file:
-    - `exampleTargetNames` now only contains `"ExampleSupport"` (still a literal declaration), with a doc comment explaining why the other four moved.
-    - Added `exampleTargetSpecsDeclaresExpectedNames()` â€” asserts `exampleTargetSpecs` contains all four expected name literals.
-    - Added `exampleTargetsMapUsesSharedDependencies()` â€” asserts the `exampleTargets` map body wires `dependencies: exampleTargetDependencies` (already proven MCPTestServer-free by the existing `exampleTargetDependenciesConstantExcludesTestServer()`) and doesn't reference `MCPTestServer` directly.
-
-    Verification (all green):
-    - `swift build` â€” clean, zero warnings
-    - `swift package clean && swift build` â€” full clean rebuild, zero warnings
-    - `swift test` â€” 203/203 tests pass, 22 suites
-    - `swift build --target EchoTool` / `FileAssistant` / `ToolPicking` / `RemoteHTTP` â€” all succeed individually
-    - `swift package describe --type json` â€” confirms all four targets resolve as `executable` at their correct `Examples/<Name>` paths
-    - `mcp__sah__review review working` â€” 0 findings
-
